resources.string: The following information can be obtained without any permissions
Authorized permissions mean apps can access this data at any time. Please regularly check app permissions and revoke unnecessary authorizations.
This app only displays information that can be technically obtained, and does not upload or transmit any data. All information collection is done locally.

The exported report will contain:

📱 No Permission Data (55 items)
 - Device hardware info (brand, model, CPU, memory, etc.)
 - System info (Android version, SDK version, etc.)
 - Network info (IP address, WiFi info, etc.)
 - Sensor info, security status, etc.

🔐 Need Permission Data (24 items)
 - Location, contacts, SMS permissions, etc.
 - Permission status and risk descriptions

Report will be saved to:
Internal Storage/Documents/WhoAreYou/

Confirm export?
Can obtain precise GPS location with meter-level accuracy
Can obtain approximate location based on cell towers and WiFi
Can continuously track location in background, even when app is not active
Can read all contacts stored on the device
Can add, modify or delete contact information
Can read all SMS messages in inbox
Can send SMS, may incur charges or be used for fraud
Can monitor incoming SMS, intercept verification codes
Can view all call records including numbers and times
Can modify or delete call records
Can activate microphone anytime to record surrounding sounds
Can activate camera anytime to take photos or videos secretly
Can read all files on storage card
Can modify or delete files on storage card
Can read GPS location from photos
Can obtain IMEI, IMSI, phone number and other device identifiers
Can use fingerprint, face and other biometric features
Can simulate user operations, may be used to steal information
Can display windows above other apps
Can track app usage time and patterns
Can read all schedules from calendar
Can add, modify or delete calendar events
Can read browser bookmarks and history
⚠️ High Risk: Can track your real-time location and movements
⚠️ Medium Risk: Can know your approximate location area
⚠️ High Risk: Continuously monitors your location, high battery drain
⚠️ High Risk: Can analyze your social network
⚠️ Medium Risk: Can tamper contact info for fraud
⚠️ High Risk: Can obtain bank verification codes and sensitive info
⚠️ High Risk: May secretly send premium SMS
⚠️ High Risk: Can intercept all SMS verification codes
⚠️ High Risk: Can learn your social relationships and call habits
⚠️ Medium Risk: Can delete call records to hide activities
⚠️ High Risk: Can listen to your conversations and ambient sounds
⚠️ High Risk: Can take photos/videos without your knowledge
⚠️ High Risk: Can access your photos, documents and all files
⚠️ Medium Risk: Can modify or delete your files
⚠️ Medium Risk: Obtain location info from your photos
⚠️ High Risk: Can uniquely identify and track your device
⚠️ Low Risk: Can directly dial premium numbers
⚠️ Low Risk: May auto-answer incoming calls
⚠️ High Risk: Can read all sensitive info in notifications
⚠️ High Risk: Extremely high permissions, may monitor all operations
⚠️ High Risk: Can monitor all network traffic
⚠️ High Risk: Can display content over any app
⚠️ Medium Risk: Knows your usage habits and app preferences
⚠️ Medium Risk: Knows your schedules and activity plans
⚠️ Low Risk: May modify your schedules
⚠️ High Risk: Knows your interests and search habits
⚠️ High Risk: Can secretly install malware
⚠️ Medium Risk: Can uninstall security software
The following information requires app authorization

This app helps users understand what device information mobile applications can access WITHOUT any permissions.
By transparently displaying this information, we aim to raise users\' privacy awareness and help them understand how their data is collected and used.

• **Information Transparency**: Displays 55+ device info items including hardware, system, network, sensors, and security status
• **Permission Display**: Shows 24 permission items with detailed descriptions of purposes and risks after authorization
• **Risk Identification**: Three risk levels (Low/Medium/High) to help quickly identify sensitive information
• **Usage Explanation**: Detailed explanation of potential uses and risks for each info item
• **Local Processing**: All data is processed locally only, no information is uploaded
• **Zero Permissions**: No sensitive permissions requested, protecting user privacy

• **Zero Data Upload**: This app does not send data to any server
• **Local Analysis**: All information collection and display are done locally on the device
• **No Permission Requests**: Only uses normal permissions allowed by the system (network status, WiFi status)
• **Open Source**: App code is fully open source, security can be verified independently

**Why can apps access this information?**
Android provides various system APIs that can be accessed without special permissions. These APIs were originally designed to help apps better adapt to devices and provide personalized services.
**Information Usage Examples:**
• Advertisers: Use device model + Android ID for targeted advertising
• Rogue apps: Infer user location and behavior patterns through WiFi information, steal privacy data
• Security research: Detect device security status and system vulnerabilities

**Protection Tips:**
• Regularly check permissions of all apps on your phone
• Be cautious when granting sensitive permissions
• Use privacy protection tools
• Review app permissions before installation
• Disable unnecessary permissions for installed apps
• Keep your device system up to date
Device manufacturer identifier. Used by advertisers for device classification and market analysis; rogue apps may exploit specific model vulnerabilities
Exact device model. Used for device value assessment, spending power profiling; some old models have known vulnerabilities
Internal device codename. Used for precise device identification; attackers may search for specific device vulnerabilities
Commercial product name. Used for market segmentation and user group analysis
Underlying hardware identifier. Used for performance optimization and driver compatibility analysis
Core privacy data! Used for cross-app user tracking, ad attribution, and account association. Only changes after factory reset
Computational capability assessment. Used to determine device performance level, game compatibility; infers user spending level
Processor architecture. Used to select correct malicious code version; determines security feature support; rogue apps may target specific architectures
Device memory capacity. Used for performance grading, app recommendation; infers device price range and user spending ability
Current available memory. Used to determine device load status; infers user habits (multi-tasking or not)
Battery status. Used to infer usage scenarios (charging may mean at home/office); advertisers use for push timing
Charging type. Infers user location: USB may indicate at computer, AC may indicate at home or office
System version. Used for compatibility checking, feature limitation; old versions may have known security vulnerabilities; rogue apps may exploit old version vulnerabilities
API level. Precisely determines available features and security; low SDK means missing new security mechanisms
Important security indicator! Determines if device has fixed known vulnerabilities. Rogue apps may exploit unfixed vulnerabilities
Communication module version. Specific versions may have baseband vulnerabilities that can be remotely exploited

Linux kernel version. Used to determine kernel vulnerability availability; old kernels may have privilege escalation vulnerabilities; rogue apps may exploit kernel vulnerabilities for higher permissions
Complete system build identifier. Used for precise system source identification, determining if official ROM; traces system tampering history
Bootloader version. Determines if device can be unlocked, if rooted
Device running duration. Infers user habits: short time may mean just powered on, long time may mean rare restarts
User language preference. Used for content recommendation, regional services; infers possible nationality or residence
Country/region code. Used for regional restrictions, content moderation; infers user\'s country
Important location inference basis! Combined with other info can infer approximate user location
Network location identifier! Can be geolocated to city level via IP database; used for regional restrictions and risk control
Important privacy data! Can infer user location type (home/office/mall/hotel); used for location fingerprinting
Router MAC address. Used for precise location (with WiFi location database); rogue apps can track user movement
Signal strength. Used for indoor positioning, distance estimation from router; infers user position in building
WiFi frequency (2.4G/5G). Used to determine network environment quality; infers router type and usage scenario
Network connection method. Used for content adaptation, traffic control; infers usage scenario (WiFi may mean at home/office)
Mobile network operator. Used for carrier partnerships; infers user\'s country/region
Bluetooth enabled status. Used for nearby device discovery; infers possible wearable device usage
Device storage capacity. Used to determine device tier; infers user spending ability (large storage = premium device)
Available storage space. Infers user habits: full storage may mean heavy photo/download usage; empty storage may indicate new device
SD card capacity. Determines if expandable storage exists; infers user storage needs
Display resolution. Used for UI adaptation, image resource selection; infers device tier
Physical screen size. Used to determine device type (phone/tablet); infers usage scenario
Pixel density. Used for resource selection, performance optimization; high density usually means premium device
Screen refresh rate. High refresh rate = premium device; used for animation smoothness judgment
Current screen orientation. Used to determine user activity (landscape may indicate watching video/gaming)
User font preference. Greater than 1.0 may indicate visually impaired or older users; used for UI adaptation
Animation speed setting. 0 means animations disabled, possibly developer or performance-focused user
Auto lock time. Infers user habits: long time = frequent phone use; short time = power saving mode
Sound mode. Infers current scenario: silent may indicate meeting/sleep; vibrate may indicate public place
Brightness adjustment method. Auto brightness may indicate convenience-focused user; manual may indicate precise control preference
Dark mode status. Enabled may indicate night use or dark theme preference
App installation count. Infers user type: many apps = heavy user; few apps = light user or new device
Browser preference. Used to identify user group characteristics; some browsers have specific user profiles
Home launcher. Third-party launcher indicates customization preference; infers technical ability
Browser identification string. Contains device model, system version, browser version; used by servers to identify devices
Device running duration. Short time = just powered on/restarted; long time = stable operation
Time display preference. 24-hour mostly used in Europe/Asia; 12-hour mostly used in US
Device current time. Used to determine user active hours; combined with timezone can infer approximate location
Date display format. Different regions have different preferences; helps determine user\'s region
Total device sensors. More sensors mean more data collection capability; some sensors can be used for user behavior tracking
Detects device motion. Used for step counting, gesture recognition, user behavior analysis; commonly used in games and fitness apps
Detects device rotation. Used in AR/VR applications, precise motion tracking; can infer user activity type
Detects magnetic field. Used for electronic compass, positioning assistance; can infer user orientation and position
Detects ambient light intensity. Used for auto brightness adjustment; can infer user environment (indoor/outdoor)
Detects object distance. Used to turn off screen during calls; can infer user habits and scenarios
Counts steps. Used for health tracking; can infer user exercise amount and lifestyle
All sensors supported by device. Used for app feature adaptation; rogue apps can leverage sensor data for user behavior analysis and tracking
Rooting reduces system security, may allow malicious apps to gain higher privileges; some banking apps may refuse to run
Debug mode may allow app data to be obtained by debug tools; should be disabled in production
SELinux is Security-Enhanced Linux. Enforcing mode provides strongest security protection; Permissive mode reduces security
ADB debugging allows computer to directly access device; may be maliciously exploited to gain device control
App has passed digital signature verification, integrity guaranteed
System file integrity is good, not tampered
